resources.string:
Local Backup

Backup to file exports your complete database as a ZIP file you can save anywhere.

Auto backup automatically saves a backup to a folder you choose whenever data changes.

Restore from file imports a previously exported ZIP. This replaces all current data.

Cloud Backup

Cloud backup stores an encrypted copy in your Google Drive. Only you can decrypt it with your passphrase.

Auto backup uploads automatically after each change.

Back up now triggers an immediate upload regardless of the auto backup setting.

Passphrase encrypts your backup. It is never stored on the device. Without it, cloud backup cannot be restored.

Up to 5 backup versions are kept; older ones are deleted automatically.

Cross-device Sync

Once connected to Google Drive, you can enable Cross-device sync to keep your data continuously in sync across every device signed in to the same backup.

Changes made on one device are pulled to the others, and you can rename or remove devices from the sync list.

Restore

Restoring a backup replaces all current data. The app will restart after a successful restore.

For cloud restore, you need the passphrase used when the backup was created.

How it works

MoneyM imports your bank transactions as suggestions — nothing is added to your books until you accept it. Rejected suggestions are never asked about again, but stay visible under Rejected.

Set up Enable Banking

Bank access runs through Enable Banking, free for personal use on your own accounts.

  • Create an account at enablebanking.com and open the Control Panel.
  • Register an application in the production environment (sandbox cannot reach real banks) and set its redirect URL to exactly https://davidvster.github.io/moneym.github.io/bank-callback.html.
  • Download the application's private key (PEM file). It is shown only once — keep it safe.
  • Copy the Application ID shown on the application page.
  • Activate the application by linking your own bank account: choose the country, your bank and the account type (personal or business), then sign in at your bank to confirm. Only linked accounts are accessible — that is what keeps personal use free.

Connect in the app

Paste the Application ID and the key contents into the Bank sync screen and tap Save and validate. Then tap Connect a bank: choose the same country and bank and approve access in the browser. You are sent back to the app automatically; if not, paste the redirect link manually.

Assign each linked bank account to a wallet so accepted transactions land in the right place.

Good to know

Bank consent is valid for up to 180 days; reconnect when the app asks.

Automatic sync runs when the app starts, at most every 6 hours. Sync now works anytime.

Only booked transactions are imported — recent card payments can show up a day or two later, once your bank books them.

Snapshot vs Tools

Snapshot sends a compact summary of the selected year to the model — totals, categories and balances for that period. It is fast, works on every engine, and is best when your question is about that specific year.

Tools lets the model search your full history and pull only the records it needs. It is best for open-ended questions that span more than one year. Some smaller models do not support tools; when that happens the app falls back to a snapshot automatically.

On-device processing

All analysis runs entirely on your device. Your financial data and your questions never leave the phone and are never sent to any server.

The trade-off is quality. On-device models are far smaller than cloud models, so they make more mistakes, miss details and reason less reliably. Smaller models perform worse than larger ones — a tiny model is fast but weak, a larger on-device model is slower but more capable. Even the best on-device model is not as good as a cloud AI.

Disclaimer

This is an experimental feature. Responses are AI-generated, may be inaccurate, and are not financial advice. No responsibility is taken for decisions made based on them.
